name: oss
description: Open Science Skills (oss). 39 Claude Code skills for experimental social science and computational text analysis (including fable-orchestrate and opus-orchestrate, general multi-model orchestration workflows led by Fable 5 or by Opus 4.8 under ultracode, and advisor, a single independent-second-reviewer consult with Fable 5 matched to the session's current reasoning effort). Research-repository scaffolding and auditing organized around a sources/ knowledge base (research-repo); conjoint design, diagnostics, and data cleaning; survey design; list experiments; cross-national design; topic modeling; LLM text classification; VLM-based OCR pipelines and OCR system comparison/evaluation (CER/WER); post-OCR cleanup; independent model-council voting and inter-rater agreement; deliberative GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus 4.8 model committees, chaired by Opus 4.8, GPT-5.6 Sol, or Fable 5 (model-committee, model-committee-sol, model-committee-fable); LLM calibration via token log-probabilities; FAIR manuscript checks; citation checking; source-claim fact-checking; literature review evidence mapping; figure design; table design; house-style LaTeX paper formatting from any draft, with a tested convert-and-build driver (paper-tex); figure/table auditing; replication-package scaffolding (FAIR + Horiuchi conventions); paper pre-submission review (single-model and cross-model Claude+Codex variants); hypothesis building; narrative building; pre-registration; methods reporting; presubmit activation; third-party journal review; and creative-divergence brainstorming in single-model and cross-model Claude+Codex variants (diverge, diverge-codex). Invoke as /oss:skill-name (or just /skill-name when there is no ambiguity) or let Claude auto-trigger based on context.
